reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need plan. Plan: modify /etc/security/limits.conf or add file in /etc/security/limits.d. Use sed to add '* hard core 0'. Ensure not duplicate. Also remove any bad config lines. Approach: read /etc/security/limits.d; if exists, choose 10-ssg-hardening.conf. If not, create. But as per rule, to modify file, readable, use read_file. We need to check if file contains line already matching pattern, if so skip adding. If not, append.

Let's propose to create /etc/security/limits.d/10-ssg-hardening.conf if not exist. Add line. Also ensure * hard core 0 not commented. Use sed -i to uncomment if commented.

Also could modify main file. Plan: check /etc/security/limits.conf, if no line, add. But better to add dropin. Provide plan. Then run review_plan.
